prompt: The repository contains a Bash permissions reconciler that operates on a simulated metadata table for a shared Linux tree. It correctly repairs the shared root and its immediate children, but entries farther down the tree are silently left with stale ownership, group, mode, ACL, and setgid metadata. Fix the focused traversal defect so reconciliation covers every regular file and directory below `shared`. Preserve the numeric owner/group policy, file and directory modes, access ACLs, default ACL inheritance on every directory, and setgid directories. Keep verification of the supplied user access matrix and the reversible rollback manifest intact. Symlinks must never be followed; any symlink resolving outside the managed shared tree, including a dangling lexical escape, must reject the whole operation before state or rollback output changes. Internal symlinks and all unrelated files and metadata must remain untouched. Do not use host accounts, privileged ownership changes, ACL tools, or the network, and do not modify the protected tests.
